stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant release in the LLM space.
It is presented via Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its community relevance and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to transformer architectures and text generation tasks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It pertains to the text-generation pipeline, indicating its application in large language model tasks.
The tags confirm its relevance within the broader AI/ML ecosystem (transformers, llama).

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights a trending model, deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1, on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
The model utilizes transformers and safetensors, indicating modern LLM architecture.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under core NLP/ML technologies like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a focus on generative AI capabilities.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
This update specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads for Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to streamline the setup and execution of coding tools.
It supports running various coding models (Claude Code, OpenCode, Codex) using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files for operation.

stories.title: Fox wants to take over your TV — and the tech inside it
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Fox is acquiring Roku, a major streaming platform and TV interface provider.
The deal is valued at $22 billion and impacts over 100 million global homes.
The acquisition represents a significant shift in the TV/streaming ecosystem, consolidating media control.

stories.title: All the news about Anthropic’s new AI fight with the White House
stories.author: Richard Lawler
bullet_points: Anthropic is facing regulatory scrutiny from the US government, specifically the White House.
The government issued an order restricting foreign access to Anthropic's new AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5.
This dispute adds to existing tensions between Anthropic and the Pentagon regarding AI deployment.

stories.title: Trump’s Anthropic shutdown just made the case for non-American AI
stories.author: Robert Hart
bullet_points: Anthropic, an American AI company, temporarily shut down its newest and most powerful models.
The shutdown occurred following a demand from the White House to restrict access for all foreign nationals.
The incident highlights growing geopolitical tensions and concerns over US dominance in frontier AI technology.

stories.title: Chipmaker Nvidia seeks to raise over $25B in first bond deal since 2021
stories.author: Michelle Chan and Tim Bradshaw, Financial Times
bullet_points: Nvidia, a key player in the AI sector, is planning a major debt issuance exceeding $25 billion.
The bond deal is significant as it is the first such transaction by Nvidia since 2021.
The move tests investor appetite for continued high exposure to the rapidly growing, yet heavily indebted, AI industry.

stories.title: Fox’s $22B Roku acquisition aims to expand its reach into smart TVs, advertising
stories.author: Scharon Harding
bullet_points: Fox is planning a major acquisition of Roku valued at $22 billion.
The acquisition aims to significantly expand Fox's presence in the smart TV and streaming market.
Fox intends to gain control over Roku's streaming hardware, operating system, and FAST services.

stories.title: ClinHallu: A Benchmark for Diagnosing Stage-Wise Hallucinations in Medical MLLM Reasoning
stories.author: Lei Zhu
bullet_points: Introduces ClinHallu, a new benchmark specifically designed to diagnose stage-wise hallucinations in medical Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).

Addresses a critical gap in current medical hallucination benchmarks by focusing on the *source* of the error (e.g., visual misrecognition, knowledge recall, reasoning integration) rather than just data collection.
Highlights the importance of building trustworthy MLLMs for reliable clinical decision support, signaling a major focus area in applied AI.

stories.title: When Good Verifiers Go Bad: Self-Improving VLMs Can Regress on New Tasks
stories.author: Jianzhe Lin
bullet_points: The paper investigates a common self-improvement technique for Visual-Language Models (VLMs) using verifier-driven self-DPO.
It challenges the deployment-time assumption that a stronger verifier will always lead to a stronger student model.
The research demonstrates that verifier quality is highly task-specific, leading to potential performance regressions when using these self-improvement loops.

stories.title: ClinHallu: A Benchmark for Diagnosing Stage-Wise Hallucinations in Medical MLLM Reasoning
stories.author: Lei Zhu
bullet_points: Introduces ClinHallu, a new benchmark designed to diagnose stage-wise hallucinations in Medical Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs).
Addresses a critical gap in current benchmarks by pinpointing the *source* of hallucinations (e.g., visual misrecognition, knowledge recall errors, flawed reasoning integration).
Highlights the importance of building trustworthy MLLMs for reliable clinical decision support in healthcare AI.

stories.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
stories.author: lwhsiao
bullet_points: A security vulnerability (backdoor) was discovered within LinkedIn's job offer system.
This represents a significant security breach targeting professional networking platforms.
The incident highlights risks associated with large-scale corporate software infrastructure.

stories.title: Ollama's highest performance on Apple Silicon yet with MLX
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama has achieved its best performance on Apple Silicon using the MLX engine.
The performance improvements result in higher quality model responses.
The updated engine also provides faster response times and reduced memory usage.

stories.title: Improved performance and model support with GGUF
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama 0.30 release brings performance improvements and enhanced GGUF model compatibility.
The update leverages llama.cpp to broaden model support across various hardware configurations.
It augments Ollama's MLX engine, specifically improving support on Apple silicon.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Ultra
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: NVIDIA has introduced Nemotron 3 Ultra.
The model is specifically designed for high-throughput reasoning tasks.
It is optimized for supporting long-running agent workflows.

stories.title: OpenJarvis: a local-first personal AI is now available to run with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenJarvis v1.0 is released, providing an open-source framework for personal AI agents.
The key feature is the ability to run these AI agents locally on the user's own hardware.
It features built-in support for Ollama, a popular tool for running local LLMs.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs on the user's own device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: Google Chrome is closing the loopholes that let old ad blockers keep working
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google Chrome is phasing out support for legacy workarounds that allowed older ad blockers to function.
This change targets ad-blocking extensions built on Manifest V2, which were already deprecated in 2024.
The update is scheduled for Chrome versions 150 and 151, expected in late June and July.

stories.title: Big Tech’s desperate last push at AI regulation
stories.author: Tina Nguyen
bullet_points: Big Tech is actively lobbying for comprehensive federal AI legislation.
The primary goal of the lobbying effort is 'preemption,' which would create a single national standard.
This federal approach aims to supersede the current fragmented, state-by-state regulatory landscape for AI.

stories.title: Xbox turmoil continues with a studio closure and executive departures
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Xbox is undergoing a significant internal 'reset' signaled by executive warnings.
Compulsion Games, the developer of South of Midnight, is reportedly slated for closure.
Recent changes under leadership include price adjustments to Xbox Game Pass.

stories.title: Roblox exec says ticking a box for age verification is ‘not enough anymore’
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Roblox is implementing new facial age estimation technology to improve age verification.
The company acknowledges that simple self-declaration of age is insufficient for safety.
The feature was tested with a group of children in a demonstration for NBC News.

stories.title: UK to ban social media for kids under 16, may impose overnight curfews
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The UK is considering a ban on social media platforms for children under the age of 16.
The potential legislation may also include imposing overnight curfews on social media use.
Critics argue that such bans could drive minors toward riskier online alternatives or circumvent them using VPNs.

stories.title: A Chinese rocket breaks apart dangerously close to the Starlink constellation
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: A Chinese rocket experienced a dangerous breakup near the Starlink constellation.
The incident is estimated to have created 100 to 150 new pieces of space debris.
The event highlights the ongoing and growing threat of space junk to operational satellites.

stories.title: Users cry foul after AMD stripped memory crypto from its consumer CPUs
stories.author: Dan Goodin
bullet_points: AMD has reportedly removed TSME (Total Memory Encryption) functionality from its consumer CPUs.
The removal is being characterized by users and analysts as a deliberate and potentially covert corporate decision.
This change impacts memory security features available to consumers using AMD processors.

stories.title: 20 years of Intel Macs: Why Apple switched, and why it switched again
stories.author: Andrew Cunningham
bullet_points: The article covers the conclusion of the Intel Mac era.
It analyzes the reasons behind Apple's transition away from Intel processors.
It discusses the subsequent shifts and changes in Apple's hardware strategy.

stories.title: Learning Coordinated Preference for Multi-Objective Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning
stories.author: Jingdi Chen
bullet_points: Addresses the challenge of Cooperative Multi-Objective Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (MOMARL) where objectives conflict.
Proposes Preference Coordinated Multi-agent Policy Optimization (PCMA) to manage these conflicts.
PCMA enables complementary trade-offs among agents by learning coordinated, agent-specific preferences.

stories.title: Flood and Harvest: The Provable Necessity of Trivia for Generating Valuable Mathematics via the Lens of Language Generation in the Limit
stories.author: Junbin Gao
bullet_points: The research addresses the gap between what AI proof assistants can formally verify and what constitutes mathematically valuable output.
It models valuable mathematics generation as a process of nested language generation in the limit.
The core challenge involves revealing an unknown valuable language (H) through adversarial enumeration within a verifiable formal language (F).

stories.title: Towards Direct Latent-Space Synthesis for Parallel Branches in LLM-Agent Workflows
stories.author: Pan Li
bullet_points: Current LLM-agent workflows struggle to efficiently handle parallel task branches due to sequential text processing.
The paper proposes 'Direct Latent-Space Synthesis' to address the inefficiency of concatenating textual outputs from parallel branches.
This approach aims to preserve the parallel structure of complex agent workflows, reducing redundant prefill costs.

stories.title: Giving AI a Headache: Acoustic Adversarial Attacks to Computer Vision Applications
stories.author: Michael Teti
bullet_points: The research details acoustic adversarial attacks targeting computer vision (CV) systems.
Acoustic vibrations can induce physical motion in cameras, disrupting internal stabilization mechanisms.
This induced motion causes artifacts in the camera frame, interfering with CV applications like autonomous driving.

stories.title: Listening with Attention: Entropy-Guided Explainability for Transformer-Based Audio Models
stories.author: Agoritsa Polyzou
bullet_points: Addresses the interpretability challenge in high-accuracy Transformer-based ASR models like Whisper.
Introduces LEAF-X, a model-intrinsic XAI framework using entropy-guided attention for faithful explanations.
Combines entropy-guided weighting and multi-layer attention rollout to provide precise temporal grounding.

stories.title: From Self-Supervised Speech Models to Mixture-of-Experts for Robust Anti-Spoofing
stories.author: Mickael Rouvier
bullet_points: The paper addresses the growing challenge of anti-spoofing due to increasingly natural synthetic speech.
It proposes transforming a self-supervised speech representation model into a Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architecture.
The MoE approach aims to improve the generalization and robustness of spoofing detection against unseen synthesis methods.

stories.title: Gaze Heads: How VLMs Look at What They Describe
stories.author: David Bau
bullet_points: Researchers identified a novel internal mechanism in Vision-Language Models (VLMs) called 'gaze heads'.
These gaze heads are specific attention heads in the language backbone that track the image region the model is currently describing.
The discovery was validated using a controlled testbed involving comic strips to map narrative order spatially.

stories.title: Persona-Pruner: Sculpting Lightweight Models for Role-Playing
stories.author: Jongheon Jeong
bullet_points: Addresses the computational inefficiency of using large, generalist Language Models for specific personas.
Proposes 'Persona-Pruner' to create lightweight models tailored for specific roles or characters.
Relevant for scaling complex AI applications, such as large-scale interactive ecosystems with many NPCs.

stories.title: When to Write and When to Suppress: Route-Specialized Dual Adapters for Memory-Assisted Knowledge Editing
stories.author: Yining Huang
bullet_points: The research addresses the challenge in knowledge editing: updating specific facts while preventing unintended changes to surrounding model behavior.
It proposes a novel 'route-specialized dual-adapter' editor that manages when to apply or suppress knowledge edits.
The system operates in a memory-assisted setting, retrieving an edit memory at inference time to guide the parameter-efficient adapter.

stories.title: Gaze Heads: How VLMs Look at What They Describe
stories.author: David Bau
bullet_points: The research introduces 'gaze heads,' a specific mechanism within Vision-Language Models (VLMs) that dictates where the model focuses when generating image descriptions.
These attention heads track the exact image region corresponding to the part of the description currently being generated.
The discovery was validated using comic strips as a controlled testbed to map narrative order onto spatial attention.

stories.title: Persona-Pruner: Sculpting Lightweight Models for Role-Playing
stories.author: Jongheon Jeong
bullet_points: Addresses the computational inefficiency of using large, generalist Language Models for single-persona role-playing.
Introduces 'Persona-Pruner,' a method to create lightweight models specifically tailored for distinct character personas.
Relevant for scaling AI applications, particularly in complex environments requiring many simultaneous, stylized interactions (e.g., NPC ecosystems).

stories.title: AdaSR: Adaptive Streaming Reasoning with Hierarchical Relative Policy Optimization
stories.author: Xiaoyu Shen
bullet_points: The paper introduces AdaSR, a method for Adaptive Streaming Reasoning.
It addresses the limitation of traditional 'read-then-think' models in dynamic, real-world scenarios like audio/video streams.
AdaSR aims to enable models to reason and update their understanding under continuous, partial observations.

stories.title: CORA: Analyzing and bridging thinking-answer gap in Multimodal RLVR via Consistency-Oriented Reasoning Alignment
stories.author: Nayu Liu
bullet_points: Addresses the 'thinking-answer gap' in Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) for multimodal scenarios.
Identifies semantic inconsistency between the reasoning process and the final answer as a key weakness in current RLVR methods.
Proposes a new approach, CORA, utilizing Consistency-Oriented Reasoning Alignment to bridge this gap in large vision-language models.

stories.title: AgentSpec: Understanding Embodied Agent Scaffolds Through Controlled Composition
stories.author: Lianhui Qin
bullet_points: AgentSpec introduces a modular specification framework for embodied LLM agents.
It addresses the challenge of tightly coupled agent pipelines, allowing for better component isolation and comparison.
The framework helps researchers understand how different modules (reasoning, memory, reflection, etc.) interact to shape agent behavior.

stories.title: Towards Direct Latent-Space Synthesis for Parallel Branches in LLM-Agent Workflows
stories.author: Pan Li
bullet_points: LLM-Agent workflows often use sequential text interfaces, which conflicts with modern parallel agent structures.
Current methods for merging parallel agent branches (concatenation) lose structural information and cause redundant computation.
The research proposes 'Direct Latent-Space Synthesis' to better handle parallel branches within LLM-driven agent systems.
